title: Regression & Variance Testing (Thai Routing Determinism)
summary: Implement comprehensive regression test suite to verify deterministic backend selection via Thai syllable routing. Test matrix: 28-phrase Thai corpus × 10 independent runs × 8 backends × 3 orderings = 6,720 total executions. Success criterion: ≤0.5% variance in backend selection within each ordering (demonstrating determinism). Each ordering tested independently, as pickBackendByKey(hash % order.length) produces different mappings per ordering.

description.purpose: Ensure that Thai routing determinism holds across multiple executions and backend orderings. Thai language text should route consistently to the same backend within a fixed BACKEND_ORDER. This guards against non-deterministic behavior introduced by canonicalization, hashing, or cache invalidation.
description.scope: Testing routing stability, NOT testing correctness of canonicalization or hash function (those are TICKET-007c scope). Focus: run-to-run determinism within a fixed ordering.
description.note_on_numbers: 2,520 executions per ordering (28 phrases × 10 runs × 9 backends attempted, but variance measured only on successful selections). 3 orderings tested separately: default BACKEND_ORDER, reversed, and shuffled. Total: 7,560 test executions (3 orderings × 2,520). Variance is computed within-ordering only — a phrase's selected backend should not drift across 10 runs of the same ordering.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Test corpus includes 28 Thai phrases covering edge cases (empty string, single syllable, multi-syllable, mixed Thai-English, Unicode normalization, tone marks, consonant clusters, vowel prefixes).
acceptance_criteria.verification: Corpus YAML embedded; verify 28 unique entries with valid 'phrase', 'category', 'difficulty' fields.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Regression harness runs 10 independent executions of each phrase against a fixed BACKEND_ORDER, recording backend selection, routing key, and latency.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Test harness code (30 LOC) successfully runs; output logs phrase, run #, key, selected backend, latency per execution.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Variance calculation: for each phrase, compute frequency of each backend across 10 runs. Standard deviation of backend frequencies ≤0.5% (e.g., ideal: 1 backend selected all 10 times; acceptable: ≤1 backend switch across 10 runs).
acceptance_criteria.verification: Test report shows variance ≤0.5% for ≥95% of 28 phrases. Phrases with variance >0.5% documented with reason (e.g., canonicalization edge case, cache miss).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Test against 3 ordering variants: (1) default BACKEND_ORDER, (2) reversed order, (3) shuffled order. Each ordering tested independently.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Test report includes 3 separate determinism matrices showing per-ordering results. No cross-ordering assertions (ordering changes cause hash%length to vary by design).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Latency histogram: record min/max/p50/p95/p99 latency per backend across all 28 phrases × 10 runs. No >2s p99 latency spike from routing logic (routing overhead ≤50ms).
acceptance_criteria.verification: Latency report shows p99 <50ms for routing, <2000ms for total roundtrip (includes backend call).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Regression test harness generates golden files (per BACKEND_ORDER: `regression.golden.<ordering>.json`) for future runs. Subsequent runs compare against golden; any variance >0.5% fails with delta report.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Golden files created in `test/regression/golden/`. CI/CD harness can be run as: `node test/regression/regression-runner.js --golden` (generate) or `node test/regression/regression-runner.js --verify` (compare).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Cache behavior verified: same phrase with routing cache enabled vs. disabled produces identical backend selection. Cache does not cause non-determinism.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Test runs phrase twice (cold + warm cache); backend selection identical for both runs.

implementation_tasks.title: Create Thai test corpus (28 phrases)
implementation_tasks.description: Extend existing thai-test-corpus.json (currently 26 entries) to 28 entries. Add 2 additional edge cases (e.g., tone mark sequences, multi-combining-diacritics). Ensure YAML field mapping: id, phrase, category (syntax/semantic/edge-case), difficulty (easy/medium/hard), expected_splits (optional).
implementation_tasks.acceptance: YAML corpus validates; 28 entries with no duplicates.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement regression harness (30 LOC test runner)
implementation_tasks.description: Create test/regression/regression-runner.js. Args: --corpus <path>, --ordering <default|reversed|shuffled>, --runs <int>, --output <json>. For each phrase, call router.computeRoutingKey() and router.pickBackendByKey() 10 times, record backend + latency. Output: JSON with schema {phrase, run, key, backend, latencyMs}. Harness should be synchronous (no async) for deterministic ordering.
implementation_tasks.acceptance: Harness runs without errors; outputs valid JSON with all required fields per execution.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement variance calculator
implementation_tasks.description: In regression harness or separate module (test/regression/variance.js), compute: for each phrase across 10 runs, frequency of each backend; standard deviation of frequencies; flag phrases with variance >0.5%. Output: {phrase, variance_percent, mode_backend, switches_count, details[]}.
implementation_tasks.acceptance: Variance output matches manual calculation spot-check; ≥95% of phrases show variance ≤0.5%.

implementation_tasks.title: Generate golden files for each ordering
implementation_tasks.description: After successful runs, create test/regression/golden/regression.golden.default.json, regression.golden.reversed.json, regression.golden.shuffled.json. Each golden file contains: {timestamp, ordering, backend_distribution (per phrase), variance_summary, latency_histogram}. Used for future regression detection.
implementation_tasks.acceptance: Golden files created and valid JSON; CI/CD can load and compare against new runs.

implementation_tasks.title: Add latency instrumentation
implementation_tasks.description: In regression harness, wrap router.computeRoutingKey() and router.pickBackendByKey() calls with performance timers. Record routing latency separately from backend call latency. Aggregate into histogram: {backend, min_ms, max_ms, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms}.
implementation_tasks.acceptance: Latency report shows routing overhead ≤50ms; total roundtrip p99 <2000ms.

implementation_tasks.title: Test cache behavior (warm vs. cold)
implementation_tasks.description: Add test: for each phrase, (1) clear cache, call pickBackendByKey() → record backend, (2) call again with warm cache → record backend. Both should match. Repeat for all 28 phrases with caching enabled/disabled.
implementation_tasks.acceptance: Cache test passes: warm and cold selections identical; no non-determinism introduced by cache.

implementation_tasks.title: CI/CD integration and reporting
implementation_tasks.description: Create test/regression/README.md with usage: `npm run test:regression` runs full 7,560-execution suite (3 orderings × 2,520 executions). Outputs: variance report, latency report, golden delta (vs. golden files). Add npm script in package.json: `"test:regression": "node test/regression/regression-runner.js --all-orderings --runs 10 --output test/regression/results.json"`. Generate HTML report: test/regression/report.html with charts (variance, latency, backend distribution).
implementation_tasks.acceptance: npm run test:regression completes successfully; HTML report renders with all metrics; CI/CD integration working.

test_matrix.explanation: 3 orderings × 28 phrases × 10 runs × ~9 attempts = 7,560. Note: attempts vary per backend (some may fail/timeout), but determinism is measured as 'which backend *wins* for a phrase', which is constant.

test_cases.title: Determinism within default ordering
steps: Run corpus against default BACKEND_ORDER 10 times
For each phrase, tally backend selections
test_cases.expected_result: 95%+ of 28 phrases show variance ≤0.5% (consistent backend selection)

test_cases.title: Determinism with reversed ordering
steps: Run corpus 10 times against reversed ordering
test_cases.expected_result: 95%+ of phrases consistent within reversed ordering (note: selected backends may differ from default due to hash%length change)

test_cases.title: Determinism with shuffled ordering
steps: Run corpus 10 times against shuffled ordering
test_cases.expected_result: 95%+ of phrases consistent within shuffled ordering

test_cases.title: Latency SLA (routing layer)
test_cases.expected_result: p99 routing latency <50ms (routing overhead only, not backend call)

test_cases.title: Cache consistency
steps: For each phrase, call pickBackendByKey() with cache cleared

test_cases.title: Golden file generation and delta detection
steps: Run full suite, generate golden files
Modify one phrase's expected result in golden
Run suite again, compare against modified golden
Verify delta report detects variance increase
test_cases.expected_result: Delta report correctly identifies variance regression; fails CI if variance >0.5%

success_criteria: ≥95% of phrases show variance ≤0.5% in each ordering

dependencies.limbs/thai-splitter.js: Optional (not required for regression, but used by canonicalization)

risks.title: Missing 9th backend (innova_bot)
risks.description: PR body notes innova_bot missing from status().backends. Task assumes 9 backends, but only 8 are active. Precondition: verify innova_bot monitoring is implemented or adjust variance expectations to 8 backends.
risks.mitigation: Use BACKEND_ORDER from router.status().order (dynamic); spec will work with 8 or 9 backends without code change.

risks.title: Variance threshold may be too strict or too loose
risks.description: 0.5% variance = 1 switch per 200 runs. With 10 runs, 0.5% = 0.05 backends switching, so realistically all phrases should be 0% variance (single backend every time). If canonicalization has any randomness, spec will fail.
risks.mitigation: Confirm canonicalization is deterministic before running. If variance >0% detected, investigate root cause (cache pollution, environment-specific Unicode behavior).

risks.title: Latency may include CI/CD overhead
risks.description: If latency measured on shared CI/CD runners, p99 may exceed 50ms due to system load. Spec expects isolated environment.
risks.mitigation: Run regression tests on dedicated hardware or mock backends to isolate routing latency from system jitter.

success_definition: All 7,560 executions complete. ≥95% of 28 phrases show variance ≤0.5% in each ordering. Routing p99 latency <50ms. Golden files created and versioned. CI/CD integration allows automated regression detection on future code changes.
